title: Doryopteris pilosa
uses: Doryopteris pilosa is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is often employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. Additionally, Doryopteris pilosa is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
taxonomy: Doryopteris pilosa is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. The family Pteridaceae is part of the order Polypodiales and the subclass Polypodiidae. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Doryopteris pilosa. The species is native to tropical regions and is often found in humid environments.

intro: Doryopteris pilosa, also known as the hairy male fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. The hairy male fern has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Doryopteris pilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to possess immunomodulatory and hepatoprotective effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Doryopteris pilosa is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
